017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.springboot;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.function.*;
024
025import org.springframework.core.env.*;
026
027/**
028 * Bridges a Spring {@link Environment} into the Juneau {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.Settings Settings}
029 * singleton as a {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySource PropertySource}.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Allows {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Value @Value("&#123;spring.property.key&#125;")} resolution
033 * against the same property sources that Spring Boot would resolve through
034 * {@code @org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Value}: {@code application.yaml},
035 * {@code application.properties}, command-line args, environment variables, and any custom
036 * {@code org.springframework.core.env.PropertySource} that Spring loads.
037 *
038 * <h5 class='section'>Lifecycle:</h5>
039 * <p>
040 * Instances are auto-registered by {@link SpringBeanStore#SpringBeanStore(org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext,
041 * org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.BeanStore) SpringBeanStore}'s constructor and removed from
042 * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.Settings#removeSource(org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySource)
043 * Settings} when {@link SpringBeanStore#clear()} is called.  Callers that want explicit lifecycle
044 * control can construct the source directly and add it via {@code Settings.get().addSource(...)}.
045 *
046 * <h5 class='section'>Lazy resolution:</h5>
047 * <p>
048 * The {@code Environment} is resolved lazily on the first {@link #get(String)} call so that
049 * constructors that build the source from an {@code ApplicationContext} do not interact with the
050 * context until a lookup actually happens.  This matters for tests that mock the context and assert
051 * "no interactions" until a Spring-side bean is requested.
052 *
053 * <h5 class='section'>Ordering:</h5>
054 * <p>
055 * Because {@code Settings} walks property sources in reverse insertion order, the Spring
056 * {@code Environment} bridge — registered when Spring creates the bean store — shadows any
057 * classpath-default {@code juneau.cfg} loaded earlier through the SPI-discovered
058 * {@code ConfigPropertySourceProvider}.  Programmatic {@code addSource()} calls made after the
059 * Spring context is built (e.g. from a user-provided microservice {@code Config}) still win.
060 *
061 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
062 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ValueAnnotation">{@code @Value} basics</a>
063 *    <li class='jc'>{@link SpringBeanStore}
064 * </ul>
065 */
066public class SpringEnvironmentPropertySource implements org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySource {
067
068   private final Supplier<Environment> envSupplier;
069
070   // Guarded by the synchronized block in get(...); volatile reads cover the fast-path check.
071   private volatile boolean resolved;
072   @SuppressWarnings({
073      "java:S3077" // Publish-once reference: env is fully resolved before its single assignment (constructor or inside the synchronized block) and is never compound-mutated, so volatile visibility is sufficient.
074   })
075   private volatile Environment env;
076
077   /**
078    * Constructor that bridges a pre-built {@link Environment}.
079    *
080    * @param env The Spring environment to bridge.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
081    */
082   public SpringEnvironmentPropertySource(Environment env) {
083      this.env = Objects.requireNonNull(env, "env");
084      this.envSupplier = null;
085      this.resolved = true;
086   }
087
088   /**
089    * Constructor that lazily resolves the {@link Environment} on first lookup.
090    *
091    * <p>
092    * Use this overload when wiring through {@link org.springframework.context.ApplicationContext}: pass
093    * {@code appContext::getEnvironment} so the application context isn't invoked until a lookup
094    * actually occurs.
095    *
096    * @param envSupplier Supplier of the Spring environment.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.  May return
097    *    <jk>null</jk>, in which case this source returns
098    *    {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult#missing()} for every key.
099    */
100   public SpringEnvironmentPropertySource(Supplier<Environment> envSupplier) {
101      this.envSupplier = Objects.requireNonNull(envSupplier, "envSupplier");
102   }
103
104   @Override
105   @SuppressWarnings({
106      "java:S2589" // Double-checked locking: the inner `if (!resolved)` and its else-branch look redundant to Sonar's single-threaded symbolic execution but are essential for MT correctness (another thread can set resolved=true between the outer and inner checks).
107   })
108   public org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult get(String name) {
109      var e = env;
110      if (! resolved) {
111         synchronized (this) {
112            if (! resolved) {
113               // safeOpt swallows exceptions thrown by the supplier — e.g. a mocked
114               // ApplicationContext that returns null or throws during getEnvironment().
115               env = e = safeOpt(envSupplier::get).orElse(null);
116               resolved = true;
117            } else {
118               e = env;
119            }
120         }
121      }
122      if (e == null)
123         return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.missing();
124      // Profile piggyback: a Juneau config-profile activation lookup resolves to Spring's active profiles, so a
125      // Spring-Boot deployment has a single source of truth for which profiles are active.  Only answered when the
126      // caller has NOT explicitly set juneau.profiles.active as a property (that exact key wins if present).
127      if (PROFILES_ACTIVE_KEY.equals(name) && ! e.containsProperty(name)) {
128         var active = e.getActiveProfiles();
129         if (active.length > 0)  // getActiveProfiles() never returns null per the Environment contract.
130            return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.present(o(String.join(",", active)));
131         return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.missing();
132      }
133      if (! e.containsProperty(name))
134         return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.missing();
135      // Spring's getProperty() returns null only for unresolved placeholders, which
136      // containsProperty() already filtered out.  Wrap defensively anyway.
137      var v = e.getProperty(name);
138      return org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertyLookupResult.present(o(v));
139   }
140
141   /** The Juneau config-profile activation key that piggybacks on Spring's active profiles. */
142   private static final String PROFILES_ACTIVE_KEY = "juneau.profiles.active";
143}
